accepted_bit_depth.implementation_consequence: Accept only unsigned eight-bit samples for this contract.
accepted_bit_depth.normative_text_summary: The base raster is eight-bit grayscale with one unsigned byte per pixel.

accepted_color_model.implementation_consequence: No automatic color conversion is justified.

accumulation_precision.ambiguity: The conformance guidance does not lock evaluation order or floating-point environment.
accumulation_precision.implementation_consequence: Accumulator precision and order remain non-normative.
accumulation_precision.normative_text_summary: The sample convolution accumulates into a double variable.

alpha_channel_treatment.implementation_consequence: Reject alpha-bearing input from the pure matrix contract.
alpha_channel_treatment.normative_text_summary: The base grayscale raster is specified without an alpha channel.
alpha_channel_treatment.value: alpha channel is not part of the base raster

border_extension_rule.ambiguity: The prose says missing taps are filled by mirroring or duplicating edge pixels; the code instead substitutes the current convolution-center pixel for every out-of-bounds tap.
border_extension_rule.implementation_consequence: Border pixels cannot have canonical outputs.
border_extension_rule.normative_text_summary: The printed prose and printed code prescribe observably different border values for nonconstant images.

certification_pathway_availability.ambiguity: The broad fingerprint-compression project is marked ongoing and the publication is maintained, but no current downsampler portal, ungated test package, or confirmed response was found.
certification_pathway_availability.implementation_consequence: Report submission and certification as not performed; do not claim present availability.
certification_pathway_availability.normative_text_summary: Current official pages preserve the guidance but do not establish an active downsampler submission service.
certification_pathway_availability.source: NIST official project and publication pages
certification_pathway_availability.value: current downsampler-specific submission availability not verified

certification_submission_procedure.ambiguity: The 2016 procedure does not by itself prove current service availability.
certification_submission_procedure.implementation_consequence: No certification claim is permitted without an actual NIST result.
certification_submission_procedure.normative_text_summary: SP 500-306 defines the submission sequence and configuration-specific evaluation.
certification_submission_procedure.value: obtain a NIST identifier, receive the gated reference image link, process the suite, and submit outputs for evaluation

clipping_or_saturation.ambiguity: No required clipping interval, saturation order, or out-of-range behavior is stated.
clipping_or_saturation.implementation_consequence: Out-of-range and floating-error handling cannot be selected.
clipping_or_saturation.normative_text_summary: The sample code constructs a gray color from an integer but does not define a canonical clipping rule.

coefficient_generation_formula.ambiguity: The sample code is not identified as the conformance reference artifact.
coefficient_generation_formula.implementation_consequence: The mathematical coefficient shape is identified, but byte identity still depends on precision and normalization authority.
coefficient_generation_formula.normative_text_summary: The report prints the Gaussian formula and sample C-sharp evaluation at integer grid distances.
coefficient_generation_formula.value: radial Gaussian evaluated at integer offsets from -4 through +4, followed by finite-kernel normalization in sample code

coefficient_precision.ambiguity: No runtime, library version, coefficient table, hexadecimal representation, or conformance artifact identity is fixed.
coefficient_precision.normative_text_summary: The sample code uses double and platform math functions.

column_parity.ambiguity: The human column terminology is not explicitly mapped to a coordinate origin in the guidance.
column_parity.implementation_consequence: The retained zero-based column origin is not authoritative.
column_parity.normative_text_summary: SP 500-289 names odd-column decimation and Appendix C labels a zero-based odd-index branch OO.
column_parity.value: described as odd-column decimation; sample code's OO branch retains zero-based odd indices

compression_scope.implementation_consequence: Compression codecs are outside the pure downsampling function.
compression_scope.normative_text_summary: The procedure addresses downsampling independently and also describes pre- and post-compression contexts.
compression_scope.value: downsampling may be standalone or adjacent to compression; this audit isolates downsampling

conformance_metric.implementation_consequence: This is a certification-level image-suite metric, not a byte-exact pixel oracle.
conformance_metric.normative_text_summary: Supplier outputs are compared through the mean spectral-image RMSD measure.
conformance_metric.value: mean RMSD of SIVV signals over the official image suite

conformance_tolerance.ambiguity: The tolerance accepts nonidentical pixel outputs and therefore cannot choose among exact pixel semantics.
conformance_tolerance.implementation_consequence: Record the limits but do not translate them into a byte-exact implementation choice.
conformance_tolerance.value: Gold mean below 0.00521; Silver mean at least 0.00521 and below 0.00631; recommendation requires mean below 0.00631

coordinate_origin.ambiguity: Odd row and odd column are not defined as one-based ordinals or zero-based indices.
coordinate_origin.implementation_consequence: Parity wording cannot be translated canonically to indices.
coordinate_origin.normative_text_summary: The guidance does not define its indexing convention.

corner_handling.ambiguity: Neither the prose nor the code resolves how two-axis overflow maps under the stated mirror-or-duplicate wording; the code uses the center pixel.
corner_handling.normative_text_summary: Corner taps inherit the same prose/code conflict as edges.

decimation_order.normative_text_summary: The 1000 ppi image is low-pass filtered before rows and columns are discarded.

even_input_dimensions.normative_text_summary: Factor-two subsampling of even dimensions yields one-half width and height regardless of parity.
even_input_dimensions.value: exactly one half on each even dimension

filter_before_decimation.normative_text_summary: The Gaussian is applied as an anti-aliasing low-pass filter before subsampling.

filter_radius.normative_text_summary: The selected Gaussian treatment uses radius four pixels.

gaussian_sigma.normative_text_summary: The selected Gaussian treatment uses sigma 0.8475.

input_smaller_than_kernel.ambiguity: No minimum dimensions or behavior for multiple simultaneous out-of-bounds taps is specified normatively.
input_smaller_than_kernel.implementation_consequence: Inputs smaller than 9 by 9 are unsupported by a canonical contract.
input_smaller_than_kernel.normative_text_summary: The sample code can execute but does not establish conformance semantics.

intermediate_rounding.ambiguity: No normative statement forbids contraction, reordering, or other intermediate behavior.
intermediate_rounding.implementation_consequence: Intermediate arithmetic cannot be made canonical.
intermediate_rounding.normative_text_summary: The shown loop multiplies and adds double values before final conversion.
intermediate_rounding.value: no explicit intermediate rounding in sample code

jpeg2000_relationship.implementation_consequence: Do not use codec defaults as the Gaussian oracle.
jpeg2000_relationship.normative_text_summary: Gaussian filtering plus decimation is the selected interoperability strategy; JPEG 2000 transcoding is discussed as a distinct performance tradeoff.
jpeg2000_relationship.value: a separate upstream compression or selective-transcoding pathway, not a substitute definition for exact Gaussian pixel semantics

kernel_dimensions.implementation_consequence: Use nine taps on each axis.
kernel_dimensions.normative_text_summary: The report defines width as two times radius plus one; radius four gives a 9 by 9 kernel.

kernel_separability.ambiguity: The formula is mathematically separable, while the sample code constructs a two-dimensional array; no required implementation structure is stated.
kernel_separability.implementation_consequence: Do not require either implementation structure as canonical.
kernel_separability.normative_text_summary: No source requires a separable or non-separable implementation.

mirrored_pixel_semantics.ambiguity: The exact coordinate mapping implied by mirroring is absent and conflicts with center-pixel substitution in code.
mirrored_pixel_semantics.implementation_consequence: Exact mirror indices cannot be selected.
mirrored_pixel_semantics.normative_text_summary: The report does not specify reflect, reflect-101, or symmetric endpoint behavior.

normalization_rule.ambiguity: SP 500-289 does not normatively require this exact operation order or precision.
normalization_rule.implementation_consequence: Exact normalized coefficients cannot be locked from guidance alone.
normalization_rule.normative_text_summary: Appendix C normalizes the generated finite kernel to unit sum.
normalization_rule.value: sample code divides every finite 9 by 9 coefficient by the double-precision sum

numeric_representation.ambiguity: The guidance does not prohibit an equivalent fixed-point implementation or define intermediate equivalence.
numeric_representation.implementation_consequence: No canonical numeric representation may be selected.
numeric_representation.normative_text_summary: Appendix C stores coefficients and the weighted sum as double.

odd_input_dimensions.ambiguity: SP 500-289 and SP 500-306 do not define odd-dimension retention, and parity itself is unresolved.
odd_input_dimensions.implementation_consequence: Odd dimensions are unsupported by a canonical contract.
odd_input_dimensions.normative_text_summary: Appendix C demonstrates different odd-size outputs among the four branches.
odd_input_dimensions.value: sample branches choose floor or ceiling according to retained parity

output_bit_depth.implementation_consequence: Output samples are unsigned eight-bit values.
output_bit_depth.normative_text_summary: The interoperability raster uses 256 gray levels in unsigned bytes.

output_height.ambiguity: The guidance does not state a general odd-height contract or authoritative retained origin.
output_height.implementation_consequence: General output height remains unresolved for odd input.
output_height.normative_text_summary: Appendix C dimensions depend on the selected parity branch.
output_height.value: sample code uses floor(height/2) for retained odd zero-based rows and ceil(height/2) for retained even rows

output_ppi_metadata.ambiguity: The target spatial sample rate is stated, but metadata preservation or encoding is not defined for the algorithm contract.
output_ppi_metadata.implementation_consequence: Defer file metadata behavior to a later integration policy.
output_ppi_metadata.normative_text_summary: The output is described as 500 ppi imagery.
output_ppi_metadata.value: target sample rate is 500 ppi; exact metadata encoding is not specified for a pure matrix

output_rounding.ambiguity: SP 500-289 does not specify rounding, and the official conformance downsampler artifact is unavailable.
output_rounding.implementation_consequence: Pixel values near integer boundaries are unresolved.
output_rounding.normative_text_summary: Appendix C passes an integer cast of the weighted sum to the color constructor.
output_rounding.value: sample code casts the weighted sum to int, which truncates nonnegative values toward zero

output_width.ambiguity: The guidance does not state a general odd-width contract or authoritative retained origin.
output_width.implementation_consequence: General output width remains unresolved for odd input.
output_width.normative_text_summary: Appendix C dimensions depend on the selected parity branch.
output_width.value: sample code uses floor(width/2) for retained odd zero-based columns and ceil(width/2) for retained even columns

png_metadata_treatment.ambiguity: PNG may carry a required identifier in a comment, but general PNG metadata copying, dropping, and PPI encoding are not specified.
png_metadata_treatment.implementation_consequence: Do not add a PNG production contract in this audit.
png_metadata_treatment.normative_text_summary: PGM is the only format accepted by NIST for conformance testing even though PNG is discussed for standalone output.

repeated_edge_vs_reflection.ambiguity: The phrase mirroring or duplicating does not select reflection, repeated-edge reflection, or edge replication, and the sample code implements none of those generally.
repeated_edge_vs_reflection.implementation_consequence: No library border mode is admissible as a default.
repeated_edge_vs_reflection.normative_text_summary: Multiple standard border interpretations remain distinguishable.

row_parity.ambiguity: The human row terminology is not explicitly mapped to a coordinate origin in the guidance.
row_parity.implementation_consequence: The retained zero-based row origin is not authoritative.
row_parity.normative_text_summary: SP 500-289 names odd-row decimation and Appendix C labels a zero-based odd-index branch OO.
row_parity.value: described as odd-row decimation; sample code's OO branch retains zero-based odd indices
